assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions represent the array below?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD3$ rows with $\purpleC5$ squares in each row.



[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD3$ rows of $\purpleC5$ is equal to $\purpleC5+\purpleC5+\purpleC5$.

We can also relate the array to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}$
$\blueD3$ rows of $\purpleC5$ is equal to $\blueD3\times\purpleC5$.

The following expressions represent the array:

- $\purpleC5 + \purpleC5 + \purpleC5$
- $\blueD3\times\purpleC5$

options.alt: An array of squares arranged in 3 rows of 5 squares each.

**Which expressions represent the array below?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

We can also relate the array to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}$
$\blueD8$ rows of $\purpleC4$ is equal to $\blueD8\times\purpleC4$.

The following expressions represent the array:

- $\purpleC4+\purpleC4+\purpleC4+\purpleC4+\purpleC4+\purpleC4+\purpleC4+\purpleC4$
- $\blueD8\times\purpleC4$

options.alt: An array of circles arranged in 8 rows of 4 circles each.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which image shows $5\times4$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate multiplication to arrays:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}$
$\blueD5 \times \purpleC4$ equals $\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC4$.

This array has $\blueD5$ rows with $\purpleC4$ squares in each row.  

[[☃ image 1]]

This image represents $5 \times 4$:  


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![An array of squares arranged in 5 rows of 4 squares each.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fca6d21b9fd805b929160b2f3be68aa5095f10fe)
![An array of squares arranged in 3 rows of 5 squares each.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c97c343fca670ee4ab915224648018cce566bcd)
![An array of squares arranged in 2 rows. There are 5 squares in the top row. There are 4 squares in the bottom row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4abf49ef4a5219fdd764106402b589594819fafb)

assessment_items.question_content:
The garden has $3$ rows, with $8$ carrots in each row.  


 **How can we find out how many carrots there are in all?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD3$ rows with $\purpleC8$ carrots in each row.  


[[☃ image 1]]

We can relate the array to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}$
$\blueD3$ rows of $\purpleC8$ carrots is equal to $\blueD3 \times\purpleC8$.

options.alt: An array of carrots arranged in 3 rows of 8 carrots each.

options.alt: An array of apples arranged in 4 rows of 4 apples each.
